items.title: USDA posts list of approved SNAP food restriction waivers with 2026 rollout dates
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published an updated table of 21 states with approved SNAP food restriction waivers and their target implementation dates. The list includes Florida (04/20/26) and Texas (04/01/26), signaling some states plan to begin restricting purchases of items like soda, candy, energy drinks, and certain desserts in 2026.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released guidance to help states implement statutory limits on federal Medicaid and CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens beginning 10/01/26. CMS noted exceptions, including continued federal matching for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to CHIP Health Services Initiatives or state options to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: HUD clarifies HOTMA interim reexams must occur when households add or remove members
items.summary: HUD’s Notice PIH 2026-09 / H 2026-05 requires PHAs and multifamily owners to conduct interim reexaminations whenever a household adds or removes a member, even if the change does not affect adjusted income. HUD also describes an exception for agencies/owners with a written policy not to conduct interim reexams for increases in adjusted income during the last 3 months of a recertification period.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: KFF updates tracker: 75.3 million enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP nationally as of January 2026
items.summary: KFF’s Medicaid Enrollment and Unwinding Tracker reports 75.3 million people enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP nationally as of January 2026. The tracker notes this is a 20% decline from March 2023 enrollment levels, but still 5% higher than February 2020 (pre-pandemic) enrollment.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts
items.summary: USDA FNS set FY 2026 WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts at $26/month for children, $48/month for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52/month for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo notes the FY 2026 inflation adjustment is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline, and FY 2026 runs from 10/01/25 to 09/30/26.

items.title: IRS reminder: file-extension request due April 15, extends filing to Oct. 15, 2026
items.summary: The IRS reminded taxpayers they can request an automatic extension to file their federal income tax return, but the request must be made by 04/15/26. The extension moves the filing deadline to 10/15/26 (it does not extend time to pay), and the IRS also notes automatic extensions may apply for certain taxpayers, including some members of the military and taxpayers living abroad.

items.title: Federal FAFSA deadlines: June 30, 2026 (2025–26) and June 30, 2027 (2026–27)
items.summary: USA.gov notes the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2025–26 school year is 06/30/26. It also lists 06/30/27 as the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2026–27 school year, though states and colleges may have earlier priority deadlines.

items.title: Social Security announces 2.8% COLA for 2026 (SSI increase starts Dec. 31, 2025)
items.summary: Social Security announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026 affecting about 75 million people. SSA says the increased payments start in January 2026 for nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries and on 12/31/25 for nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients.

items.title: IRS posts updated EITC maximums for tax year 2025 (up to $8,046 for 3+ kids)
items.summary: IRS EITC tables list maximum EITC amounts for tax year 2025 of $649 (no qualifying children), $4,328 (1 child), $7,152 (2 children), and $8,046 (3+ children). The same tables list an investment income limit of $11,950 or less for tax year 2025.
